---
title: "Plan media features without running them"
subtitle: "Inspect OCR, face, CLIP, DINO, and video-audio requests before execution."
sidebar: cookbook
---

## Use when

You want to know what a media feature would do before installing optional dependencies or touching large files.

```{python}
from natural_features.workflows import available_features, plan_features

media = available_features(
    modality=["image", "video"],
    budget="allow_python",
)
[entry.feature_id for entry in media if entry.feature_id in {
    "video.audio.extract",
    "image.ocr",
    "video.ocr",
    "vision.face",
    "vision.clip",
    "vision.dino",
}]
```

## Plan the requested features

```{python}
plan = plan_features(
    "video",
    features=["video.audio.extract", "vision.face"],
    budget="allow_python",
)
plan.to_recipe()
```

## What the plan tells you

The plan names the extractor for each feature, the kind of input it will
receive, and the parameters it will use. Producing this plan does not decode a
movie or load a neural model. A valid plan shows that the requested inputs and
outputs fit together; it does not prove that every optional package or model is
installed.

## Strict variant

```python
plan_features(
    "image",
    features=["vision.clip"],
    budget="allow_python",
    feature_params={
        "vision.clip": {
            "execution_mode": "strict",
            "strict_dependency": True,
            "local_files_only": True,
        }
    },
)
```
